message:
fix(net,http): prefer earliest header terminator, validate chunked trailers, misc hardening

WebSocket client + handshake (client.rs, handshake.rs):
  The header boundary scanner now finds both CRLF (\r\n\r\n) and LF
  (\n\n) positions and picks whichever comes first, rather than the
  previous logic that tried CRLF then fell back to LF. This prevents
  misparse when a response body happens to contain the other terminator
  sequence before the actual header boundary. The handshake parser gets
  the same fix. URL parser refactored from if-else to map_or_else.
  Test added for earliest-terminator preference.

Chunked encoder (stream.rs):
  - encode_chunk_into now returns early on empty data instead of emitting
    a zero-length chunk (which is the stream terminator). Previously an
    empty data slice would produce "0\r\n\r\n", prematurely ending the
    chunked stream.
  - encode_final now validates trailer header fields via
    validate_header_field before writing them to the wire, skipping
    malformed names/values that could enable header injection.
  - Test added: chunked_encoder_skips_invalid_trailer_fields.

HTTP client (http_client.rs):
  - Self type alias used for recursive TlsTunnel enum variant
  - execute_single future wrapped in Box::pin to avoid unbounded
    future size from recursive redirect handling

TLS (acceptor.rs, connector.rs):
  - Minor clippy-driven improvements to pattern matching and control flow

Web middleware:
  - Extended middleware chain with additional validation hooks

IO write_ext test:
  - Fixed write_vectored test assertion: Vec's write_vectored writes all
    buffers (11 bytes total), not just the first non-empty one (6 bytes)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
